mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and factory slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances when industrial production contracts sharply.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate forces tighter real monetary conditions, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >55% of observations when inflation exceeds 2.5% alongside stable or rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window or payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns.

mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth falling below a 1.5% annualized threshold signals weak aggregate demand and recessionary risks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of cases when GDP growth drops below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial output and employment indicates robust productive capacity utilization and labor absorption, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in industrial production and payrolls yields negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations.
